title: To permit the use of artificial intelligence in arbitration, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This policy aims to allow the use of artificial intelligence in arbitration processes, potentially modernizing and streamlining dispute resolution. It may also address other related purposes not specified in the given title.

title: To increase access to artificial intelligence through programs of the Department of Agriculture, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.
summary: This policy aims to make artificial intelligence more available through programs run by the Department of Agriculture. It will likely achieve this by implementing new or modified programs.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a legislative policy aimed at regulating AI models, although the specific details of its purpose, scope, and requirements are not immediately available. This policy likely focuses on the development, deployment, and accountability of AI models in various sectors or industries.

title: A bill to amend the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to enable schools to teach elementary school and secondary school students about the dangers, limitations, and responsible use of artificial intelligence technologies, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to amend the education act to teach students about the dangers and responsible use of artificial intelligence. It is designed to inform students about the limitations and potential risks of AI technologies in educational settings.

title: Liquid Cooling for AI Act of 2025
action: Committee on Energy and Natural Resources Subcommittee on Energy. Hearings held. With printed Hearing: S.Hrg. 119-366.
summary: The Liquid Cooling for AI Act of 2025 is a policy aimed at implementing or improving liquid cooling systems for artificial intelligence systems to prevent overheating. This policy likely includes guidelines or regulations to promote and ensure the safe operation of AI systems.

title: To establish a pilot program for use by U.S. Customs and Border Protection at land ports of entry along the Arizona border to assess the use of artificial intelligence through an anomaly detection algorithm, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Border Security and Enforcement.
summary: This policy aims to create a pilot program that uses artificial intelligence to detect anomalies at U.S. Customs and Border Protection land ports on the Arizona border. The program will serve to assess the effectiveness of this technology in this particular area.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act proposes a temporary halt on new artificial intelligence data center construction to address concerns about energy consumption and environmental impact. The policy aims to assess and address the growing demands of the AI industry on the power grid and natural resources.

title: Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: The Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act is a policy that likely aims to support and advance the use of artificial intelligence in small businesses. This act seeks to enhance the growth, innovation, and competitiveness of small businesses through the utilization of AI technology.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: The Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act is a proposed law intended to evaluate and mitigate the risks of artificial intelligence used for malicious purposes. The exact specifics of the law are not specified, leaving its implementation and details open to interpretation and further discussion.

title: To require artificial intelligence chatbot providers to provide data privacy and security, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This policy aims to establish requirements for artificial intelligence chatbot providers to ensure they protect users' data and keep it secure. The policy also covers additional purposes related to regulating AI chatbots.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This 2026 legislation, the Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act, is likely aimed at addressing the potential environmental effects of artificial intelligence.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: This act aims to protect families from the financial burden of energy costs associated with artificial intelligence data centers. Its name suggests an attempt to mitigate the environmental and economic impact of these data centers.

title: Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: The Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026 aims to address issues surrounding aging populations in relation to the increasing use of artificial intelligence.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to promote the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in the financial services sector to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and customer experience. This act likely seeks to create a regulatory framework that balances innovation with security, stability, and consumer protection.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
summary: The 'Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act' aims to remove regulatory barriers and encourage the use of artificial intelligence in the financial sector to improve efficiency and services. This act seeks to strike a balance between promoting innovation and ensuring the stability and security of financial systems.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: This policy aims to protect consumers from being misled by artificial intelligence. It likely establishes regulations and safeguards to prevent deceptive AI practices.

title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to require the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a prize competition program relating to artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This document proposes amending a 2020 act to require the establishment of a prize competition program for artificial intelligence. The proposed amendments aim to further advance artificial intelligence initiatives and related purposes.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy document, the Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026, outlines a plan for managing risks associated with artificial intelligence at a federal level.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: The Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act aims to evaluate and mitigate potential terrorism risks associated with generative Artificial Intelligence. This legislation likely seeks to identify and address potential security threats posed by AI technology.

title: NSF AI Education Act of 2025
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 33 - 0.
summary: The NSF AI Education Act of 2025 is a policy aimed at promoting education and research in artificial intelligence through the National Science Foundation. This policy likely aims to invest in AI education, research, and development initiatives to advance science and technology in the field.

title: LIFT AI Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 34 - 1.
summary: The LIFT AI Act is likely a policy document that aims to advance or improve artificial intelligence in some way. More information is needed to provide a specific summary, but based on the title, it could focus on lifting limitations or barriers to the development and use of AI.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act aims to create a legislative framework to regulate and oversee the development and use of artificial intelligence in the workforce. The policy's specifics are not available, but it likely seeks to balance AI innovation with worker welfare and data protection.

title: CREATE AI Act of 2025
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 29 - 0.
summary: The CREATE AI Act of 2025 is a legislative proposal aiming to regulate and standardize the development and use of artificial intelligence technology in the United States. The exact details of the policy remain unclear due to the lack of an available description.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act is a policy that likely seeks to temporarily halt or limit the construction of new artificial intelligence data centers. The exact intentions and details of the act are unknown due to the lack of description provided.
